fund.name: Aditya Birla Sun Life Medium Term Plan

allocations.rationale: 10.5% 3-year return (pre-tax) for predictable income, low volatility.
21.3% 5-year return (pre-tax), equity-taxed for better post-tax growth in 30% slab.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera. Step 2: Transfer ₹80L to the selected funds (Quant Multi Asset Allocation Fund, Aditya Birla Sun Life Medium Term Plan, Nippon India Multi Cap Fund). Step 3: Start investing ₹75k/month as lump sums (e.g., 1st of each month) into these funds.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm all investments are active. Check if any fund has underperformed its category.
After 1 year: Rebalance if any fund’s allocation deviates by >10% from target.
After 4 years: Reassess for the daughter’s wedding fund (short-duration debt portion).
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, adjust allocations to maintain 50% conservative hybrid, 30% debt, 20% equity. Sell underperforming funds and buy others to restore balance.
plan.rationale: Scarlett’s conservative risk appetite and 12-year horizon call for stability. The conservative hybrid (50%) ensures capital safety, short-duration debt (30%) targets the wedding fund, and equity (20%) leverages tax benefits for long-term growth. This mix balances preservation and growth, aligning with her goal to transfer wealth securely.
risks: Market Risk: Equity funds may drop 20–30% in bad years, but the 20% allocation is small and tax-efficient.
Interest Rate Risk: Debt funds may lose value if rates rise, but short-duration funds are less volatile.
Liquidity Risk: Avoids illiquid assets; all funds are easily redeemable.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.
